title: Rock bolt with strain detection means
abstract: The invention provides a strain measuring rock bolt (10) which includes a yielding rod (12) of a malleable material which extends between a distal end (14) and a proximal end (16), a bore (18) which extends through the rod between the distal and proximal ends, an strain sensor (24) comprising an elongate member (26) of a conductive elastomeric material, which is fixed to the rod (12) within the bore to elongate as the rod elongates under load, and a power source which is electrically connected to the member in a power circuit, a processing module electronically connected to the power circuit, wherein the processing module is adapted to measure a change in an electrical property of the elongate member, as the member elongates under strain and wherein the processing module is adapted to calculate the strain on the rod based on the change and to communicate with an output means based on the calculated strain.
claims: Claims 1. A strain measuring rock bolt which includes a yielding rod of a malleable material which extends between a distal end and a proximal end, a bore which extends through the rod between the distal and proximal ends, a strain sensor comprising an elongate member of a conductive elastomeric material, which is fixed to the rod within the bore to elongate as the rod elongates under load, and a power source which is electrically connected to the member in a power circuit, a processing module electronically connected to the power circuit, wherein the processing module is adapted to measure a change in an electrical property of the elongate member, as the member elongates under strain, and wherein the processing module is adapted to calculate the strain on the rod based on the change and to communicate with an output means based on the calculated strain. 2. A strain measuring rock bolt according to claim 1 wherein the conductive elastomeric material of the elongate member Is a silicone carbon composite material 3. A strain measuring rock bolt according to claim 1 or 2 wherein the electrical property is resistance, capacitance or inductance. 4. A strain measuring rock bolt according to any one of claims 1 to 3 wherein the output means is a means which visually, audibly or by data communicates the difference. 5. A strain measuring rock bolt according to claim 4 wherein the output means is an LED, an audible alarm or a transmitter. 6. A strain measuring rock bolt according to claim 5 wherein the processing module communicates with the LED, alarm or transmitter, to illuminate, sound or transmit data respectively, when the calculated strain exceeds a predetermined limit. 7. A strain measuring rock bolt according to claim 5 or 6 wherein the LED is positioned to be visible from the proximal end of the rod. 8. A strain measuring rock bolt according to any one of claims 1 to 7 wherein the processing module is electronically interposed between the power circuit and the output means. 9. A strain measuring rock bolt according to any one of claims 1 to 8 wherein the processing module includes a microcontroller with an Analog-to-Digita! Converter.
